title: TabNet attention sidecar for race-group OOF diversity
summary: Train a small TabNet classifier with race-grouped folds and use its OOF predictions only as a low-weight diversity sidecar.
rationale: The public TabNet notebook reports weaker standalone OOF than GBDTs, but its sequential attention and sparse feature selection may make errors different enough to help a stack on specific regimes. This is distinct from TabM/TabPFN because the inductive bias is attentive feature masking, not MLP ensembling or foundation-style inference.
implementation_hint: Use GroupKFold by Race or Year_Race, categorical embeddings for Driver/Compound/Race, and only a compact feature set with lagged rolling pace, tyre degradation, and position deltas. Export OOF/test probabilities and test rank/logit blend weights capped at a small maximum such as 5-10%.
expected_effect: Probably weak alone, but may add a small decorrelated signal if blended only where OOF says it helps.
risk: The cited notebook’s OOF is low; TabNet can overfit and adds dependency/runtime cost. Kill quickly if its OOF rank correlation with stronger models is high or its blend weight goes to zero.
